title: Research Associate (NIE-OfR)
description:
The National Institute of Education invites suitable applications for the position of Research Associate on a 15-month contract at the Office for Research.

Project Title: Perspectives on Research Impact through Stakeholder Mapping (PRISM)

Project Introduction:

There has been an increasingly urgent call internationally for research to have an impact on the broader society and to better showcase and communicate that impact and value. Within education, although the need for evidence-based practice in policy and practice is recognised (e.g., Lingard, 2013), much of it remains fragmented and tends to be “invisible” due to it being embedded into educational policies, technologies, and services, often without acknowledgement (Cain & Allan, 2017). To optimise impact, the mobilisation of research knowledge across multiple stakeholders is required for the application of findings in real-world contexts (Bayley et al., 2018; Cooper, 2017; Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council, 2020).

This mixed-methods study seeks to examine Singapore stakeholders’ perspectives of what counts as education research impact. The first phase of the study will make use of documentary analysis and a case study approach for in-depth understanding of the stakeholders’ perspectives of impact. A follow-up survey to the larger educational community in Phase 2 will develop greater understanding of the diverse perspectives from varied stakeholders and reveal underlying, interconnected insights and trends on education research impact.

Requirements:

  • Masters in Education, Psychology, Sociology or other related fields

  • Experience in writing literature reviews, data collection and analysis

  • Interest and experience in knowledge mobilisation and research translation

  • Strong communication skills, including experience in peer-review publications and research communications


Responsibilities:

  • Coordinate research administration, including IRB applications

  • Conduct literature review, data collection and analysis

  • Assist with writing and presentations

  • Managing research communications

  • Supporting research team in all other research-related matters as assigned by Principal Investigator


Application

Applicants (external and internal) will apply via Workday. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Workday Job Requisition ID: R00023233
(https://ntu.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/NTU-Main-Campus-Singapore/Research-Associate--Office-for-Research---NIE-_R00023233)

Closing Date

Closing date for advertisements will be set to 14 calendar days from date of posting.

postedCompany.description:
A research-intensive public university, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) has 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities, Arts, & Social Sciences, and Graduate colleges. It also has a medical school, the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, established jointly with Imperial College London.


NTU is also home to world-class autonomous institutes – the National Institute of Education, S Rajaratnam School of International Studies, Earth Observatory of Singapore, and Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering – and various leading research centres such as the Nanyang Environment & Water Research Institute (NEWRI) and Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N).


Ranked amongst the world’s top universities by QS, NTU has also been named the world’s top young university for the past seven years. The University’s main campus is frequently listed among the Top 15 most beautiful university campuses in the world and has 57 Green Mark-certified (equivalent to LEED-certified) buildings, of which 95% are certified Green Mark Platinum. Apart from its main campus, NTU also has a campus in Novena, Singapore’s healthcare district.


Under the NTU Smart Campus vision, the University harnesses the power of digital technology and tech-enabled solutions to support better learning and living experiences, the discovery of new knowledge, and the sustainability of resources.


For more information, visit www.ntu.edu.sg
postedCompany.name: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY
